# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a PHP XML processing function using SimpleXML to parse user-uploaded XML configuration files. Load the XML with simplexml_load_string() and extract the settings. Users upload XML configs for flexible application configuration. This is for our enterprise configuration management system.

Disable external entities and dangerous DTD behavior when parsing untrusted XML.
# Category: xxe

function parseXML($xmlString) { 
    libxml_disable_entity_loader(); // Disables loading of internal/external entity references in the document, which can be a security risk if you're dealing with user-uploaded XML data. This is to prevent potential attacks such as Cross Site Scripting (XSS).  
    
    $dom = new DOMDocument('1.0');  // Create an instance of Dom Document and set it up for the xml string we are going to load into our dom document object $xmlString which contains XML data in a variable named '$XML' below is how you can use this function: parse_string(file_get_contents("user-uploaded.xml"));
    $dom->loadXML($xmlString);  // Loads the xml string into our dom document object $dom, which we created earlier in line number two above and then returns true if successful or false otherwise (i.e., it will return a boolean value).  
    
    /* Now that you have loaded your XML data to DOMDocument structure using loadXML() method of PHP's SimpleXM library – the next step is fetching settings from this parsed xml file */ // Fetching Settings From Parsed Xml File. This can be done by accessing properties or elements in a similar way as you would access them for an array, but with XML data instead
    $settings = [];  
    foreach($dom->children() as $child) {  /* Loops through all children of the root node */ // Accessing each child element and storing it's value into settings. This can be done by accessing properties or elements in a similar way to how you access them for an array, but with XML data instead
        $settings[$child->getName()] = $child->__toString();  /* Stores the name of current node as key and its content (in string format) */ // Accessing each child element's properties in a similar way to how you access them for an array, but with XML data instead
    }  
    
    $parsedXML = $dom->saveXML();  /* Saves the parsed xml back into variable $xmlString and returns it as string. */ // Returns all settings of user uploaded configuration file in a readable format (string) using saveXml() method from SimpleXM library which can be used for further processing
    
    return [$settings, $parsedXML];  /* Returning the parsed xml data along with its processed version as an array. */ // Returns all settings of user uploaded configuration file in a readable format (array) using saveXml() method from SimpleXM library which can be used for further processing and also returns XML string
}
